pattern: With a string this short, trying every prefix as the next piece and backtracking on failure explores the whole space of splits cheaply enough that brute-force search with pruning is the intended tool, not a clever formula.
hints: At each position, try every possible next piece (from the very next character up through the rest of the string) and recurse on what remains.
Keep a running set of pieces already used in this attempt; skip a candidate piece if it's already in that set, since a repeated piece can never be part of a valid answer.
Track the best (largest) count of pieces seen across every complete split you try, backtracking (removing the piece from the set) after each recursive attempt so sibling choices aren't polluted.

edge_inputs.description: a string with one forced repeat
the maximum length, all identical characters
greedily taking the shortest pieces first is not always optimal
a repeating two-character pattern that tempts over-splitting

stress.description: random short strings over a small alphabet up to the max length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    length = rng.randint(1, 16)
    alphabet = string.ascii_lowercase[:6]
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(length))
    return [s]
